tested_source.note: Only the executor-seam regression test was added; no product code changed. frontmatter.rs is pristine.

source_log_manifest.note: Frozen, ordered manifest of the 5 diagnostic log basenames + SHA-256 (11 total emissions) used for the investigation. Pins the source population so live log growth cannot silently change the fixture.

log_to_fixture_extractor.note: Consumes source_log_manifest.json as a FROZEN FILE LIST (not a live directory glob), verifies each file's SHA-256 (exits non-zero on missing file or hash mismatch), then COMPUTES all classification fields from the TRACING-RENDERED reply= field (not raw bytes). Fields: rendered_reply_len, has_frontmatter_delim, has_yaml_fence, matched_backend_notice, rendered_reply_sanitized. Invoked with --manifest and --logs-dir.

evidence_excerpt_emitter.note: Emits sanitized CORRELATED log excerpts from the frozen hash-verified manifest logs. For each executor.rs:610 parse-failure it joins the nearest preceding executor reply-debug line (same run_id+step, yielding session_id and reply_chars) and the nearest preceding ACP completion line with that session_id — a session_id + file-adjacency join grounded in executor.rs emission order (ACP-complete -> executor.rs:508 initial/correction reply -> executor.rs:610 parse-failure). All 11 emissions correlate. Answers the user's 'do we have logs indicating this case?' with direct, per-turn-correlated evidence.

lifecycle_trace_generator.note: Evidence-preserving manifest-backed step-lifecycle generator (--run only). For a run handle it emits, with source basename + line number and parsed sanitized fields: ALL step starts (including intervening different-step visits), replies, parse failures with shape, and the runtime RUN-ERROR/LOADED-RUN/RESUME lines with fields PARSED from the log (error kind/step/counters, status, current_step) — nothing hard-coded. A same-step re-dispatch is labeled retry only when it immediately follows a same-step parse failure with no intervening different-step start (runner.rs:221-231); the emitted row states that proof. StepRetrying records live in the mutable events/<run>.json, not these frozen logs; not fabricated.

private_source_logs.reproducibility: source-labeled investigator evidence only; private, not committed. The extractor hash-verifies the 5 manifest files, so re-running --manifest/--logs-dir reproduces genuine_emissions.sanitized.json byte-for-byte or exits non-zero with an attributable missing/changed-source error.

investigation_conclusion: The high volume of 'agent response is missing YAML frontmatter' errors is caused by NO-RESULT agent replies funneled into one misleading diagnostic. Log->fixture->summary chain (all executable) over the diagnostic logs: 11 genuine executor parse-failure emissions across 5 runs; parser_recoverable_frontmatter_count=0; 8 unambiguous backend notices (7 stall + 1 stream-close) and 3 ambiguous incomplete_preamble_or_prose (which carried no parseable result but cannot be proven incomplete from their tracing-rendered bytes). At the provider-neutral Client::prompt seam all failing turns are StopReason::EndTurn + text with no '---' (PromptTurnActivity is private to cowboy-agent-acp, consumed inside prompt()), so a no-result reply is indistinguishable from an ordinary completed prose reply lacking frontmatter; no structured incomplete-turn signal exists. The ESTABLISHED defect is the misleading CLASSIFICATION: parse_frontmatter_output runs unconditionally (executor.rs:609) and every no-result reply collapses to Error::MissingFrontmatter. The existing retry nudge already requests a complete replacement result, so the nudge wording is NOT an established defect and its delimiter guidance is not shown to cause non-convergence. Grounded fix: an accurate generic no-result classification (e.g. 'agent reply did not contain a workflow result') distinct from MissingFrontmatter, preserving recoverability; the nudge is optional to adjust and must keep the frontmatter requirement. Regression test at the execute_agent/Client seam asserts (a) POSITIVE user-visible 'did not contain a workflow result' message, (b) distinct variant from MissingFrontmatter, (c) preserved recoverability; it fails before the fix on (a).

investigator_command_records.subject: no_result_reply_gets_accurate_no_result_diagnostic_not_missing_frontmatter
investigator_command_records.exit_meaning: expected: libtest exits 101 because the executor-seam regression test fails before the fix — execute_agent returns Error::MissingFrontmatter (message 'agent response is missing YAML frontmatter') for a no-result reply, so the positive diagnostic assertion (a) fails.
investigator_command_records.purpose: Run the executor/client-seam regression test (positive no-result diagnostic + distinct variant + recoverability) and confirm it fails before any product fix.

investigator_command_records.subject: log-to-fixture-extraction
investigator_command_records.exit_meaning: exit 0: the manifest-based extractor hash-verifies the 5 frozen source-log files and regenerates the committed sanitized fixture byte-for-byte, independent of live log growth. Fields computed from the tracing-rendered reply= field; rendered_reply_len is the rendered-field length, not original bytes. If any manifest file were missing or its SHA-256 changed, the extractor exits non-zero (attributable), never silently drifting.
investigator_command_records.purpose: Provide the manifest-pinned log->fixture transform linking the frozen, hashed source logs to genuine_emissions.sanitized.json.

investigator_command_records.subject: fixture-to-summary-aggregation
investigator_command_records.exit_meaning: exit 0: aggregator over the committed sanitized fixture regenerates the committed summary byte-for-byte (11 failures / 5 runs; parser_recoverable_frontmatter_count=0; 8 notices + 3 ambiguous).
investigator_command_records.purpose: Provide the fixture->summary transform, computing the 8-unambiguous + 3-ambiguous breakdown and the zero-recoverable count.

investigator_command_records.subject: direct-log-evidence
investigator_command_records.exit_meaning: exit 0: the emitter regenerates the committed sanitized evidence excerpts byte-for-byte from the hash-verified manifest logs. Output is per-turn CORRELATED: each executor.rs:610 parse-failure is joined to the nearest preceding executor reply-debug line (same run_id+step -> session_id, reply_chars) and the nearest preceding ACP completion line with that session_id. All 11 emissions correlate. The stall-notice turns (reply='Anthropic stream stalled...') correlate to 'agent step: initial reply ... session_id=<redacted> stop_reason=EndTurn reply_chars=57' and 'ACP prompt turn completed ... stop_reason=EndTurn activity=Text', tying the 57-char no-result reply to a nonempty EndTurn text turn with no --- block; the stream-close turn (review_rca, reply_chars=76) and the prose/preamble turns correlate the same way.
investigator_command_records.purpose: Answer the user's 'do we have logs indicating this case?' with direct, sanitized, reproducible log evidence that the frontmatter parser runs on no-result replies and mislabels them MissingFrontmatter.

investigator_command_records.subject: retry-accounting-lifecycle-trace
investigator_command_records.exit_meaning: exit 0: the evidence-preserving generator regenerates run_lifecycle.txt byte-for-byte, each row carrying source basename+line number and parsed fields. It emits ALL step starts: L182 START plan (visit 1); L9341 reply plan reply_chars=5442 (parsed OK); L9342 START review_plan (intervening different-step visit); L36020 reply review_plan reply_chars=7355 (parsed OK); L36021 START plan (visit 2); L54214/L54215 reply+PARSE-FAIL prose/preamble (visit-2 initial attempt, no retry slot); L54223 START plan retry step_retries_used=1 (immediately follows L54215 same-step); L61537/L61538 stall PARSE-FAIL (retry 1); L61539 START plan retry step_retries_used=2; L67863/L67864 stall PARSE-FAIL (retry 2); L67865 RUN-ERROR kind=retry-exhaustion step=plan counters=2/2 (parsed from the error reason) -> Failed; L67905 LOADED-RUN status=Failed current_step=plan steps_executed=3; L67906 RESUME status=Failed current_step=plan; L67909 START plan (visit 3); L76618/L76619 stall PARSE-FAIL; L76620 RUN-ERROR kind=retry-exhaustion step=plan counters=2/2. The two durable retry slots were consumed by the L54223 and L61539 retries; the intervening review_plan visit proves L36021 is a new visit-start not a retry. review_plan's routing status is NOT in the tracing log, so no changes_requested claim is asserted.
investigator_command_records.purpose: Make the retry accounting reproducible: map each parse failure to visit/attempt, show which two dispatches consumed the 2 retry slots, and emit exhaustion + resume from a manifest-backed procedure.

investigator_command_records.subject: notice-literal-presence
investigator_command_records.command: grep tool (pattern 'stream stalled|stalled while waiting|stream closed before a terminal|terminal response event', path crates)
investigator_command_records.exit_meaning: the specialized grep tool over crates/ returns only the literal embedded in this investigation's own regression-test fixture (executor.rs test); no PRODUCT-code author of the notice strings. Establishes exact-literal absence in product source only; it does NOT attribute the strings to a specific provider.
investigator_command_records.purpose: Establish (via the repository's specialized search tool, not shell grep) that the notice literals are not authored in product code, narrowly, without over-claiming provider origin.

investigator_command_records.subject: acp-signal-flow-and-nudge-path
investigator_command_records.command: read crates/agent/client/src/traits.rs (Client::prompt -> Result<StopReason>), crates/agent/acp/src/client.rs (PromptTurnActivity 74-108 private/consumed; continuation 494-544; stop_reason default 668), crates/workflow/agent/src/executor.rs (parse call 609; retry nudge build 408-415), crates/workflow/agent/src/prompt.rs (build_retry_nudge 61-78), crates/workflow/engine/src/runner.rs (reason=last_error.to_string() 240,250)
investigator_command_records.exit_meaning: confirmed: only StopReason crosses Client::prompt; PromptTurnActivity is private to ACP and consumed inside prompt(); the executor calls parse_frontmatter_output(&visible) unconditionally and appends build_retry_nudge on attempt>1; build_retry_nudge ALREADY requests a complete replacement result ('Do not redo the work. Re-emit your result now as a complete replacement ...'); the runner feeds error.to_string() back as the retry reason. The established defect is the classification, not the nudge wording; no evidence the delimiter guidance caused non-convergence.
investigator_command_records.purpose: Ground the feasible fix contract (generic no-result classification) and correct the earlier over-claim that the nudge merely asks to re-delimit.

All 11 emissions correlate. Examples: (stall) 'ACP prompt turn completed session_id=<redacted> id=4 stop_reason=EndTurn activity=Text' + 'agent step: initial reply run_id=<redacted> step=plan session_id=<redacted> stop_reason=EndTurn reply_chars=57' + 'executor.rs:610: ... reply=Anthropic stream stalled while waiting for the next event'; (stream-close) reply_chars=76, step=review_rca, reply='OpenAI responses stream closed...'; (prose) reply_chars=2054, step=plan, reply='The reviewer requires a replan. Let me i… <prose redacted>'. The initial-reply line is now present in the deterministic output and associated to its parse-failure.

investigator_evidence_records.subject: worked-example-acp-to-parser-triple
investigator_evidence_records.observed_result: For the REPORTED run ca3f4e0a step plan (frozen log cowboy.2026-07-21.1631015.log, SHA-256 72b2c0094d1e... matches manifest), the emit_evidence_excerpts.py output contains the correlated ACP->reply->parser triple for one failing turn: 'ACP prompt turn completed session_id=<redacted> id=4 stop_reason=EndTurn activity=Text' + 'agent step: initial reply run_id=<redacted> step=plan session_id=<redacted> stop_reason=EndTurn reply_chars=57' + 'executor.rs:610: ... reply=Anthropic stream stalled while waiting for the next event'. reply_chars=57 == length of the stall notice == the visible string parsed at executor.rs:609 -> find_frontmatter_open finds no ---newline -> Error::MissingFrontmatter (frontmatter.rs:112-114). Scope: this record covers ONLY the ACP/reply/parser correlation for one turn; visit/attempt/retry-counter/exhaustion/run_failed accounting is INV-EV-08 (INV-CMD-07).

investigator_evidence_records.subject: retry-accounting-lifecycle-trace
investigator_evidence_records.observed_result: trace_run_lifecycle.py reproduces run_lifecycle.txt byte-for-byte; every row carries source basename+line number and parsed fields. Reported run ca3f4e0a: L182 START plan visit1; L9341 reply reply_chars=5442 (parsed OK, no parse fail); L9342 START review_plan (intervening different-step visit); L36020 reply review_plan reply_chars=7355 (parsed OK); L36021 START plan visit2; L54215 PARSE-FAIL prose/preamble (visit-2 initial attempt, does NOT consume a retry slot); L54223 START plan retry step_retries_used=1 (proof: immediately follows same-step parse failure L54215, no intervening different-step start); L61538 PARSE-FAIL stall; L61539 START plan retry step_retries_used=2; L67864 PARSE-FAIL stall; L67865 RUN-ERROR kind=retry-exhaustion step=plan counters=2/2 (parsed from error reason) -> Failed; L67905 LOADED-RUN status=Failed current_step=plan steps_executed=3; L67906 RESUME status=Failed current_step=plan; L67909 START plan visit3; L76619 PARSE-FAIL stall; L76620 RUN-ERROR kind=retry-exhaustion step=plan counters=2/2. Two durable retry slots consumed by the L54223 and L61539 retries; reported exhaustion sequence = 1 ambiguous prose/preamble + 2 stalls; the 4th parse failure (L76619) is a post-resume re-exhaustion, NOT part of the original 2/2. The intervening review_plan visit is emitted as proof that L36021 is a new visit-start; review_plan's routing status is not in the tracing log so changes_requested is not asserted.

adjacent_test_to_migrate.current_behavior: feeds plain "not frontmatter" prose and asserts Error::MissingFrontmatter plus that the prompt window is left closed.
adjacent_test_to_migrate.required_migration: under the generic no-result classification, the plain-prose reply carries no workflow result and must receive the new no-result category; the fix must update the classification expectation WHILE PRESERVING the prompt-window-closure assertion.
